intro: Frostia calliandrae, also known as Calliandra, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Calliandra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Calliandra is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
